name: Don Jr.
subject: Peter, I saved this message just for you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
Peter, it's Don Jr. I wanted to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude for your unwavering support. My father and I know that this fight isn't possible without patriots like you who are willing to stand up for what's right.

We're honored to have you on our team, Peter.

**[As a small token of our appreciation, I'd like to share a special message with you. Can you take a minute to view it? >>](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/79ur7CdkupGdE0_SK6R21REotunmMHisgI4liSDMOPSNYOTpXS_5hrPWc-5YYH1DpXe95Sl1w326txIbTuvNgKcVcXLxsthBPebzraVieFq8E1GkPIMsdDjZuhmdhddfT1EcG2SSxAUdFnkAGCjR-snpdB-7x-lp3Eh9lzte3mYQ9AcC6gE3PRXz-qMiZ8B6Ux0BEiLjS60YBWL88_XLcjp68-Mw0ODAEeXE7xx5nLi-4o5LhjllNXj1p4GJsx4cjZUMix86VlHd-4BIINgTFZOfqqopOxcs2Ea6h-H0rMCccCxop8q4BKsG1dTPHKrYtzWxhCjVPULUQ7hpVedb7l30O-t4rLgf2QocxSMElu2knFxWXiOp_SNXHSJh7jErdEHdkrQQlNIoncqKEcFwMbJ--tKUXGKykgpGUNwPa03NJmWo-KOt32CCNwX9T1Ps8m0Z-EdGYGPvXEauqb1Q7MjER-5ZoyTCR_el2fqM2tDNY5uWYz9CcuHkhnf3_tZ1rjl150_yfigtfq9N7K1-4kLCUck7AocQkbNuXo2r1xFqir79D6xb-HXKYXmXPB6VlNfvH2GupVh-mjzeJagOnNM2npCopGsXOoHFjUmc342Ln02uGfsXzNvuAYqferEET3rSgg801x8skgbjaRMeB7qCKeOVJu-wPTouOoQ_II-nMJzY3D2lDlO_ugat2PsDQIQFdjn48z_8psav_pjQb5HtfsZnaNXUne6zDNOpYoU_yWARSSJepH-hzurhbCqU6HKB2qChhcruc-G6ohRXIC1TIg17YkghoxStSt4GBwIsIhG7iy7kZVOLqAD4L5_Y7j7p37rxDSOvZpHUO7jTEZTcTOMaA8k8IKFtAjivWKH92lYwI3IOSHE-gUjLUU-Stc2awtqicHo92OsPAuii2VX1jY54vWFS23dm7vQbr22RwoIIJL5e0STUWcfPEejlO5MpRtDX0XeLVcNhqL8xld3Q6P71_XcbsqwJz-piw0C-lCFeD8Zso8x8PHTE-w2t9L-K-uzrz5bRbVo6YNIbOUW4LKKwLy5iCb1Fdocb5R97N_bh_n_mUo2WJSO-nPdTAhqjhw9TJw/toLu74GZjQj4ukZWC4BEj7VFMfiqbma0/16)**

Hey folks,
**Joe Walsh** and I sat down for one of those conversations that leaves you both fired up and exhausted. We got raw. We got worried. And we didn't sugarcoat a damn thing. This one is about power, violence, drift, and what kind of country we're becoming -- right now.
Here are a few moments you don't want to miss:
* [**00:01:07**] Joe lays it out bluntly: the exhaustion, the fear, and the sense that the country is drifting somewhere dangerous
* [**00:05:52**] We walk through the Minneapolis ICE shooting -- why "just obey" is a lie, and how untrained power turns deadly
* [**00:15:01**] J.D. Vance, January 6 pardons, and why calling Americans "terrorists" is a deliberate strategy
* [**00:28:01**] What Democrats actually _can_ do right now -- protests, elections, and getting one hand back on the wheel
* [**00:41:10**] The economy, collapsing job growth, AI, automation, and why neither party is ready for what's coming

This episode covers a lot -- ICE violence, executive overreach, foreign policy insanity, the economy, and the quiet panic a lot of people are feeling but don't know how to name. It's for anyone who feels overwhelmed, angry, scared, or just tired -- but still refuses to give up. If you value honest, urgent conversations like this, help me keep them going.

With urgency,
--Mike

Here are a few moments you don't want to miss: [00:01:07] Joe lays it out bluntly: the exhaustion, the fear, and the sense that the country is drifting somewhere dangerous [00:05:52] We walk through the Minneapolis ICE shooting -- why "just obey" is a lie, and how untrained power turns deadly [00:15:01] J.D. Vance, January 6 pardons, and why calling Americans "terrorists" is a deliberate strategy [00:28:01] What Democrats actually can do right now -- protests, elections, and getting one hand back on the wheel [00:41:10] The economy, collapsing job growth, AI, automation, and why neither party is ready for what's coming This episode covers a lot -- ICE violence, executive overreach, foreign policy insanity, the economy, and the quiet panic a lot of people are feeling but don't know how to name. It's for anyone who feels overwhelmed, angry, scared, or just tired -- but still refuses to give up. If you value honest, urgent conversations like this, help me keep them going. ** With urgency, --Mike | A guest post by| Walsh is a former U.S. Congressman and conservative talk radio host who waged a Republican primary challenge against Donald Trump in 2020. He is currently the director of The Social Contract and host of the "White Flag with Joe Walsh" podcast. Mike Nellis is a Democratic strategist and entrepreneur who has raised over $1 billion for Democratic campaigns and causes. He 's the founder of Authentic.org, an award-winning fundraising and advertising agency, and a former Senior Advisor to Vice President Kamala Harris. This Substack is where he writes with endless urgency about the issues of the day--and how we can save the Democratic Party and our democracy. name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: ICE must be held accountable
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20260109-1806-foote&t=1001&akid=1806%2E342411%2Ev1ko5k ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Since Donald Trump took office, ICE has been unleashed like a force of
terror on our streets, violently arresting people, raiding communities,
and leaving neighborhoods in chaos and fear. Their unchecked presence
spreads intimidation and violence at every turn, making clear that no one
is safe from their reach.

And now, the unthinkable has happened.

In Minnesota, Renee Nicole Good was recklessly shot multiple times and
killed by an ICE agent in her car. DHS claims that the ICE agent acted in
“self-defense,” but the video and eyewitness testimony show that this is a
bold-faced lie.

Leaders across the country warned that ICE’s unchecked escalation would
lead to tragedy — yet no one listened.

This administration has shown time and again that it does not care about
our safety, our families, or our neighborhoods. They will beat protesters,
terrorize observers, and even take innocent lives to push their
destructive agenda.

We cannot — and will not — stay silent. Children should be able to play
outside, families should be able to live without fear of being torn apart
or targeted, and neighbors should be able to walk their streets without
the constant threat of violence.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/jan-2026-abolish-ice/?source=em20260109-1806&t=1002&akid=1806%2E342411%2Ev1ko5k ]Will you join us now and sign our petition to Abolish ICE and get them
off our streets before more lives are lost? ICE must be held accountable.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/signup/jan-2026-abolish-ice/?source=em20260109-1806&t=1003&akid=1806%2E342411%2Ev1ko5k ]ADD YOUR NAME

Our communities deserve safety, justice, and peace.

Thank you,

The Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1806%2E342411%2Ev1ko5k

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated equally among: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

---
Fellow Conservative,

If someone sneaks into our country – or even becomes a citizen – and then brazenly defrauds the American government and hardworking taxpayers out of hundreds of millions of dollars... they should face the ultimate consequence: **deportation after stripping their citizenship.**

Don't you agree that the individuals in Minnesota who ran massive fake child care and food scams – **pocketing over $250 MILLION meant for hungry kids while buying luxury cars, homes, and properties abroad** – deserve to be held fully accountable?

It's outrageous that politicians turned a blind eye while this fraud exploded under weak oversight. Resignations aren't enough – the perpetrators need to face justice to the fullest extent, including _**revoking their naturalized citizenship and sending them back**_ where they came from.

**[That's why I support bold policies to make this happen, and I want your input right now.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=f941e4ad4f23442392d8751aaa8bd6c2&_e=hRH1RqJfs8-0K0--vtj7cm6ucxrFYWjO2ZVx7mfXGDZNa4E3ppwP_tcMs1IBUaFPzHLJGuNeJgNKtnO3dMIxsIZzvmVFxOsiyg3yo1FiPeCw71kwxwYqyVrOxrCIaoH91WyqvjT2Fr3NVZF6x685rPnGbVnNN2HLc72drE4NBE9m5NYFcFBXaqMzoRCDEk2KRQlnX5unHy5VGHFWLLbzYVfWJddoQObH5dxoaZBnPMSFQR6wZ05xWlHRN_UcRv5LZYbaC1IfWjlXxMHJpEpaRmEOAIl_hH5ODdVg9S3TTgcD6rS28nuXHG0nhJ6vFyuNxtfOiIXhFWAZ2xfGeINcdK_pZuFU0Ee1ynfiBLW7USQEZl2tcSEjzXK1YgfjT0ljhZPV0VnVCF3jJFTTIiuPet7TwVlTwAEORCpIiMbBZk6W2GuWiGIEnhQuntZX-8P0OQaUJed705zvpQDMKtJ1zqKaMqfyeGtkCuXn1M6jBrh--oJ_rs1-5klj_rKkr9DfiDGtpoCar6WVKAPoQbk6eY4IEDQbXQhi5GKvENlSKaYjf_Tpmre4zQ38tnB6gork4v6nseZrLxzHTXWyrzSGOX0ZsTq-4tOZ_84uActTM-qR32lTF0S1vHFV16I4t1pGB3hOxeXnfs3DmeHIm855E75rwn7KGXyAe49devLxNvF_AnAc7mJGBIlZbOwnWahcDp2lDl_Yf4l4CVzUUEgChZHgQAesxuQmFSvrgD8SViik3K2sQQEOnZHcAy5LYmDkEq7rbEM_U5KcfqJU0dmv6Q%3D%3D)**

# [News & Updates]()
---
| |
#### **If abolishing ICE wasn’t your position before, it needs to be now.**
---
| | Long before the Trump Administration unleashed its current reign of terror, the infrastructure for a militarized ICE was built by establishment politicians who lacked the courage to stand up. For years, advocates, organizers, and our own communities here at home warned about the increasing militarization of immigration enforcement.
Our leaders in Congress ignored those warnings. During Doris Matsui's twenty years in office—under both Republican and Democratic leadership—funding for immigration enforcement **TRIPLED**. And after all that time and all those votes, we are no closer to comprehensive immigration reform nor a pathway to citizenship for our loved ones.
Now those same politicians express remorse in the wake of the murder of Renee Good. But remorse doesn't undo two decades of inaction. Remorse doesn't protect the families being terrorized by masked agents operating without accountability and with an unlimited budget.
_**The status quo isn't enough. I’m in this race because we need representatives who will fight for our communities.**_ We can't wait for another election cycle of empty promises. We need leaders who will act—today.
**In Solidarity** _**Mai Vang, MPH**_
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=522f1f1ff4&e=dd5af5b7b1)
| | |

### **Sign Mai’s Nomination Petition to Run for Congress**

Did you know there are two ways to get on the ballot for Congress? You can write a big check and buy your way in—or you can earn it by collecting 1,714 signatures from voters in the district.
We’re doing it the People-Powered way and need your support
[**Attend a signing event near you!**](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=4ec41b7021&e=dd5af5b7b1)
---
| [SIGN THE PETITION](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=10f3d2e8c2&e=dd5af5b7b1)
---
| | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=994b09a14a&e=dd5af5b7b1)
| | |
### **Something Is Brewing Inside the Democratic Party**

On Tuesday, CADem delegates successfully submitted petitions to remove Doris Matsui from the consent calendar for endorsement.
On Wednesday, for the first time in two decades, the Sacramento County Young Democrats declined to endorse the incumbent—with 18 members voting for Mai, _zero_ for Matsui, and the majority choosing to take no position.
The momentum is real. The question is whether we can sustain it.
[**Pitch in $10, $15, $50 to help us build on this energy.**](https://maiforus.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe5f60ccb8fb671f0c1857dc9&id=6462cff46f&e=dd5af5b7b1)

name: Team Haley
subject: We need to do this right
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
Haley is determined to hear from you, null

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)
| null, it’s an election year, and Team Haley is working around the clock to build a winning campaign that can keep Michigan blue and help flip the Senate.
---
**But to do this right, we need your voice at the table, null.**
Haley is 100% committed to staying focused on the issues that matter most to regular folks. That’s why we’re asking again: **Will you take our 2026 Priorities Survey before midnight tonight?**
We even made you a personalized link: **[surveys.signforgood.com/Stevens-Election-Year-Priorities/null](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11614?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=2&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)**
It only takes a minute, but your input makes a huge difference. Thanks, null.
Team Haley
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Haley Stevens**
Date: Wed, Jan 07, 2026
Subject: I need to hear from you, null
| | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11619?t=3&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)
---
Hi null,

I’ve spent my career fighting for Michigan families. And with the help of this grassroots team, we’ve made incredible progress:
* I introduced the Stop Trump’s Abuse of Power Act after Trump illegally sent the military into U.S. cities.

* I led the charge to bring semiconductor manufacturing back to Michigan.

* I introduced the No Tariffs on Groceries Act to help bring down prices at the checkout line.

* I filed articles of impeachment against RFK Jr. for putting lives at risk and undermining public health.

I know there’s still more work to do in 2026 and beyond. That’s why I’m running for Senate. And as I fight for every corner of Michigan, I want to make sure this campaign reflects the issues that matter most to you, null.

**Your priorities are my priorities.** So before I hit the trail again, I hope you’ll take just a minute to fill out this quick 2026 Priorities Survey. **_[I need your response by midnight, so I know what's important to you:](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11620?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)_**
| | [Take the Survey](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11620?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)
---
With grit and gratitude,

Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11621?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11622?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=7&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=8&akid=1894%2E49239%2E37AzaS)
Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.
33717 Woodward Ave #539
Birmingham, MI 48009

He’s pressuring and threatening public outlets to silence all journalism that exposes the corruption of his administration and disagrees with it.

**New Dems are fighting back in Congress, but we’re asking top Democrats like you to join us in support of the First Amendment by standing up for critical media watchdogs like NPR!**

Trump’s public broadcasting cuts are still under legal review and his attacks on the First Amendment are escalating! He’s pressuring and threatening public outlets to silence all journalism that exposes the corruption of his administration and disagrees with it. To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTMzOTM2NDIS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjM4MDEwODkxNgS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/2zZrMNe80- We’re running out of time, Peter. Hundreds of people from around the country have stepped up today for Giving Tuesday, but we’re still running short of the 1,000 members we need to unlock an additional $100,000 for our work before midnight tonight. And with the Trump administration accelerating some of the most extreme anti-environment, pro-polluter attacks in modern history,falling short is simply not an option. We’re asking you, Peter: will you renew your LCV membership right now while membership is only $5 to help us unlock an additional $100,000 to oppose the Trump administration’s attempts to exploit our public lands and roll back climate progress? https://links.messages.lcv. This isn’t just about hitting a number. It’s about having the resources we need to challenge these increasingly reckless orders and attempts to shred environmental safeguards — and stop the polluter handouts that Trump and his allies are fast-tracking. RENEW YOUR MEMBERSHIP >> https://links.messages.lcv. Every single person who steps up today is critical to our efforts — here’s why this Giving Tuesday is so important: Trump is trying to erase the scientific and legal foundation of climate action. By dismantling the Endangerment Finding, which affirms that greenhouse gas emissions harm our health and welfare, Trump’s EPA would hand fossil fuel companies a free pass to spew climate pollution into our air. He’s also ending the program that required big polluters to report their pollution, making it nearly impossible to track what they’re spewing or hold them accountable.
Decades of established pollution protections are being peeled away. More than a dozen rules that keep toxic pollution out of the air we breathe and water we drink to protect our health are on the chopping block, tossed aside to allow fossil fueled power plants to cut corners and shift the burden of their damage onto our communities.
The Trump administration is giving away the Arctic to corporate polluters, working to allow more drilling in one of the planet’s last intact ecosystems. Mining roads carved through national parks and preserves. Fire-sale auctions in critical ecosystems. Hundreds of millions of acres of U.S. waters off Alaska potentially opened to drilling. It could do irreparable damage to wildlife, our climate, and Alaska Native communities. We’re working every day with our 30 state affiliates, organizing on the ground, exposing backroom deals and corruption, rallying voters, and defending the treasured and unique places that define our country. But meeting this moment requires resources — especially as we go up against big polluters and fossil fuel opponents. This $100,000 match challenge, and how many people step up this Giving Tuesday, will determine how hard we can push back. Peter, your renewal right now powers our work to protect people and the planet. Will you renew before MIDNIGHT TONIGHT to help keep this movement strong when it matters most? $5 is all it takes. https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/x_yzTg- Every contribution brings us closer. And the clock is ticking. Thanks for standing with us, Chris Brooks
Vice President of Membership
League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thanks for your support.

name: Sarah McBride
subject: This is a major step toward protecting the lifesaving care people depend on
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
We need to keep up the pressure.
#
I’m reaching out from D.C. with some good news:
**Yesterday, the House passed legislation to extend the Affordable Care Act tax credits for three more years!**
Since January 1, millions of people have seen their health insurance premiums skyrocket because Republicans failed to renew these subsidies before they expired on December 31.
Passing this legislation is a major step toward protecting the lifesaving care people depend on. The voices of folks in Delaware and across the country made this possible. That momentum helped push a few of my Republican colleagues to join us in doing the right thing.
**[But we still need to keep up the pressure. Will you chip in $10 or anything you can to help make sure this bill passes the Senate?](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1358?c0=mcb-em-20260109-1.ysvNN0&refcode=em20260109-2670&t=1&akid=2670%2E21716%2Esgqn2x)**
Extending these tax credits is one piece of the puzzle in ensuring that people can get the care they need.
I ran for office because of my experience as a caregiver to my husband Andy when he battled terminal cancer. Despite losing his life, we knew how lucky we were to have health insurance. But no one’s access to care should depend on luck.
That’s why I was proud to vote for this bill, legislation that will keep costs down and help families stay covered. And it’s why, as long as there is air in my lungs, I will fight to make universal healthcare a reality for every person.
[**If I can count on your support to keep up the pressure on the Senate to act and fuel our fight for universal health care, please chip in $10 or anything you can today.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1358?c0=mcb-em-20260109-1.ysvNN0&refcode=em20260109-2670&t=2&akid=2670%2E21716%2Esgqn2x)
**[Donate $10 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1358?c0=mcb-em-20260109-1.ysvNN0&refcode=em20260109-2670&t=21&akid=2670%2E21716%2Esgqn2x)**
Let’s keep going,
Sarah McBride
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20260109-1&t=22&refcode2=2670_21716_sgqn2x&akid=2670%2E21716%2Esgqn2x)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

name: David Hogg
subject: Greenland
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
The chaos out of Trump’s White House is insanity.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260109-1&t=1&akid=3032%2E587540%2ESEPbSg)
Peter,
On the heels of his attacks in Venezuela, Donald Trump is already talking openly about seizing Greenland by any means necessary, including military force.
What timeline are we living in? The chaos out of Trump’s White House is insanity.
Americans don’t want more “forever wars.” Even Trump knows this. It’s literally what he ran on. We don’t want more imperialism. And even the people who voted for Trump didn’t vote for him to instigate international conflicts.
What this shows us is what many of us have known from the start. Trump ran on the idea of destroying many institutions and checks on power because they allegedly didn’t serve the people. While our institutions certainly do have their flaws, the most important thing they do is provide a check on people like the President. Now, ignoring those checks Trump is using his power not to help the people but to start new wars.
This moment demands more than simply taking back the House. It demands a better Democratic Party. We are committed to building that party by directly challenging some of the most complacent and least imaginative members of
Congress, those who offer endless explanations for why they cannot confront Trump, but few ideas for what they actually _will_ do. Claiming paralysis because they are in the minority. Yet Republicans have never accepted minority status as an excuse for inaction. We need to build a party of fighters, not folders.
To those members of congress who say we can’t do anything I have one message:
Retire and pass the torch to someone who is ready to lead in this moment of crisis or be challenged by them.
**We’re working to elect a new generation of progressive leaders who will focus on making our lives better and our country stronger, not starting more wars.**
**[Peter, if you believe in the same future that we do, will you pitch in $5 to power our work? We are changing the game and the face of leadership.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260109_1_greenland.zRqgCk&refcode=em20260109-3032&amount=5&t=2&akid=3032%2E587540%2ESEPbSg)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20260109_1_greenland.zRqgCk&amount=5&refcode=em20260109-3032&t=21&akid=3032%2E587540%2ESEPbSg)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20260109-1&t=22&refcode2=3032_587540_SEPbSg&akid=3032%2E587540%2ESEPbSg)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=23&akid=3032%2E587540%2ESEPbSg)

name: Mark Kelly
subject: I will not give up on our country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
It has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.
| Folks,

If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. It didn’t stop when the FBI wanted to talk to me and other members of Congress. Because now the Department of Defense has announced that it is escalating its investigation into me.

All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear.

If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, folks.

I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution.

There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None.

If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming.

And if you’d be willing to help support me, please:

[**Split a donation between the Voter Protection Project and me. It would mean a lot.**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100958?t=6&akid=22549%2E878179%2E0FYc7m) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Mark Kelly and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/100959?t=7&akid=22549%2E878179%2E0FYc7m)

Thank you,

Mark
878179
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=22549%2E878179%2E0FYc7m). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=22549%2E878179%2E0FYc7m).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=22549%2E878179%2E0FYc7m) to request fewer emails. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution. | Folks, If you can believe it, things didn’t stop when Trump called for my hanging. It didn’t stop with the threat of a court-martial from Pete Hegseth, either. It didn’t stop when the FBI wanted to talk to me and other members of Congress. Because now the Department of Defense has announced that it is escalating its investigation into me. All for saying things the president didn’t like to hear. If you’re feeling like this isn’t the country you grew up in, I don’t blame you. But I hope you aren’t giving up on it, folks. I certainly haven’t. This country has given me too much, and I’ll never be able to repay that debt. I still believe in our democracy, our values, and our Constitution. There’s no chance in hell that somebody like Donald Trump is going to stop me from serving the people of this nation. None. If he thought all of this was going to shut me up, he’s got another thing coming. name: Alex Kelloff
subject: Clean water is a basic necessity, not a bargaining chip
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alex Kelloff for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak]Team — For 60 years, Pueblo, Otero, and neighboring counties have waited for the Arkansas Valley Conduit to be finished so they can turn on the tap without worrying about radium and uranium in their water.
Now that promise is being broken.
Last week, Trump vetoed the Finish the Arkansas Valley Conduit Act after it unanimously passed Congress with bipartisan support — legislation that would finally deliver safe, reliable drinking water to our district.
Then, my opponent Jeff Hurd and Rep. Lauren Boebert led an effort to override the president’s veto that ultimately failed on Thursday. With every Democrat voting in favor of this effort, the responsibility falls on House Republicans’ shoulders.
I’m asking you to help me fight back and demand clean water for Southeastern Colorado. Rush a $20 donation to stand up to this kind of retaliation once and for all » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak?amount=20]
RUSH DONATION » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak]
Let us be honest: Trump’s veto is a targeted act of retaliation against Colorado. It’s no secret that Trump is using federal infrastructure funding as a weapon in his personal "revenge tour" against our state.
By blocking this project, the administration is holding the health of Southeastern Coloradans hostage and harming our agricultural communities to settle a political score tied to the Tina Peters case.
The Arkansas Valley Conduit is already 35 percent locally funded. So calling this a “handout” insults the taxpayers who’ve already put more than $200 million on the table. Stranding that investment isn’t fiscal responsibility — it’s politics at its worst.
Our communities shouldn’t be collateral damage. We deserve the clean water we were promised and leaders in Washington who put people over politics.
The right to clean water is a basic necessity, not a bargaining chip. If you agree with me, support my people-powered campaign by giving $10 or whatever you can spare today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak?amount=10]
FIGHT BACK » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-ak]
I won’t stop fighting until the Arkansas Valley Conduit is finished and every family in our district has the clean water they were promised.
Thanks for being part of this fight to flip CO-03 blue.
— Alex

Forgive my persistence, but I've reached a point where I have to move, and I desperately need to know your thoughts before I do.[READ MY DECISION NOW
](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/ug1lGexPKJ6-Gdb4wOPw6qEtso5OiUlY6VnBV-30XDfvl5S-giZ1HkmUeRbChyfYicvGv0tLb-Q97z7RMzE-5iWxY68kEBcv7jXIjrf3XIgbeNOvOKRSd_-jORoZDiEY4V9EwF6-84kGVEqkTGEJLKHozHj4E_fOO1FCZaVyPtL076_dqhrmda5bfMKHtyl8QFhZyKT9kA9zYPNhlMX7D_iR4oJcQNFbHOy4XLKyEholJ-D4E6EZiNDMmJOoNAZ1PJYXdGUmdvvOyY4iL1yc83geIn6afUQNRV61eePxh44jh49-voR7GoE31nRX1ieyOcPB6EopIybHDgEHjEhcz7cKZ9Bu21CL4vDKpTKZK2XB9FStolRlHD_J41X-SGxJn7FdDFlOUkie4u7ht5KkjzNqL7Q3cFHUwJ476AoKLH9FPXmXOgu8siyGrg_dj5bIacqXEe0SVfq6tf08kP_5xSyNoMWWNeh171b8Psg1QtN7Ufd3Ozqb6yQ6NKwCz0qTnjNEpX5oo68vHd687cwS29C_zb7Pak_6aenHdYmJiRxqf4_P4ByQ22rc0kC1Fdmg1Rsa7wXstrxWybAAhUyfFZz_fHAwFi_NFdV-dLEhiSlBiWOx_c2VsRW2ps6WMY7VjDM-Raz7NOjtrRGcLg1m805PoRAZc8x_raMbfj1Sfh7fAprJtLSS81MRZMd7nJfi6xgnHf1G_lDciVNqz2QxBlExBQTyqmJTicgKjCo7ytEyPAfowIS__aSQzTRKbehGAML_NQigwl0cUFLuMpi57ZRQ-qtKdzw7LB3hhsShjyRmommQZQ/FfW6WXhs7fVKWqxsRGTjv18-GXQ9bwt_/16)I have spent long hours in prayer and conversation with those I trust most. I know leadership means accepting the heaviest burdens, and this choice has been the weightiest of my career.
I've looked at the data, I've listened to the concerns of families in your state, and I've finally reached my conclusion. I am sharing this news only with a select few today.
The fate of our movement depends on the leaders we choose to follow and the lines we choose to draw. I have drawn mine. clean_body: sent you a message earlier that I cannot allow to go unanswered. Forgive my persistence, but I've reached a point where I have to move, and I desperately need to know your thoughts before I do. have spent long hours in prayer and conversation with those I trust most. I know leadership means accepting the heaviest burdens, and this choice has been the weightiest of my career. I've looked at the data, I've listened to the concerns of families in your state, and I've finally reached my conclusion. I am sharing this news only with a select few today. The fate of our movement depends on the leaders we choose to follow and the lines we choose to draw. I have drawn mine. unwavering resolve, Mike Pompeo | | | | PAID FOR BY CHAMPION AMERICAN VALUES. NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE. 8136 Old Keene Mill Road Suite A300 Springfield, VA 22152 |

name: Democrats.org
subject: Pummeled…
body:
---
Derek, $7 — that’s the donation we’re asking you to make directly to the DNC _today._ Here’s why it matters: It’s officially midterm season, and our work at the DNC is already well underway. Last year, we saw our early investments in state parties and organizing programs pay off in a big way, with Democrats winning and overperforming up and down the ballot across the country. **This year, the stakes are even higher.** Democratic candidates like Jon Ossoff and Sherrod Brown are already being pummeled with attack ads from Republican opposition. These ads don’t just impact the candidates they’re directed at — they impact Democrats running down-ballot, too. That’s why it’s critical that we break through the noise _now._ We have to start reaching voters early to talk about the issues that matter to them. But that work can’t happen without a massive influx of resources. **That’s why we’re asking for your help today. We’ll put your contribution to work immediately supporting Democratic candidates around the country and making sure they’ve got the resources to push back against Republican attacks.** [**If electing Democrats up and down the ballot in November is important to you, we hope you’ll consider pitching in $7 or whatever you can to the DNC to help us invest in this work early.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?amount=7&refcode=em_20260109_5730_fr&refcode2=5730_22930333_xnH23H&akid=5730%2E22930333%2ExnH23H) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260109_5730_fr&refcode2=5730_22930333_xnH23H&akid=5730%2E22930333%2ExnH23H)**

clean_body: Peter, Senator Jim Risch here with something that should alarm every American: Democrats are actively opposing measures to prevent non-citizens from voting in our elections. They're fighting against verifying citizenship. They're fighting against cleaning voter rolls. They're fighting against basic election security. I've introduced legislation to strengthen election security, and Democrats have blocked it at every turn. Every American, regardless of party, should want secure and transparent elections. The fact that Democrats oppose basic security measures tells you everything you need to know. I'm fighting for election integrity in the Senate, but I need patriots like you backing me up. Thank you, Jim Risch | Paid for by the NRSC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. |

Dear Peter,

I wanted to write a personal note to you. I hear your concerns about the bottom-of-the-barrel politics in America right now. But as Alaskans, we know that **real leadership** and **selfless service** can bring us together to achieve great things.
---
I've never shied away from a tough fight, but I'd much rather keep focused on the priorities that bring us together - _like defending our personal freedoms, keeping the Alaska promise alive for our children and grandchildren, and of course, making life a little easier._ I'm proud of the relationships I've built in Juneau to break the partisan logjam and achieve results for our state. Serving you and Alaska as Lieutenant Governor has been the honor of a lifetime.
---
I'm running for governor to continue this great progress, not disrupt the system for the sake of making noise. We need an experienced leader, not "my way or the highway" attitudes. Join my team today and help me break what's left of the gridlock in Juneau and bring thousands of disillusioned Alaskans off the sidelines. **I hope you can send at least $1 to add your name to our list. $1 is all we need to show our strength as a team.** **[https://secure.anedot.com/nancy-dahlstrom-for-governor/](http://links.mail.nancyforalaska.com/u/click?_t=070db738c7d848a188c7a9e8a29f1bde&_m=ec58919d463f404bbfbbb0768648fd3d&_e=LB152aQQbFjh-GGdF2SJFVRG6wOO9Gi252gtCgkq44wCnsfW8XUOoGO7KdDT7opaXWNpjUS1_1REb_aXkaTqQXtHheHfSj_7IXFCf0T7CIJDU7GXqEQPqHGWJd1nk03Qe_bljOj_gyjcoZyRYqcQOXxmlGCFEN7P5MRG3RE4qG6b3v4wEsTfszc42cylC0dXhQWqQ7Z1qkxW8IXv9D3J39l4C3vRnXMaf0XQ0FLH3yHo58nOimksUCARfIm_4nFNK67PEvBD8mSs-gxuj4a8enrlMBCFkOFV1T9b2TIWsxq1Ep9fBUMDOUxwNQa6NJfcIKgODxaTwsWR4LWydZ5U49RaPYfzHpHCFBbCJ6ntB9CkgHuLngKCe7Y-LJWj_ueCjXh_EOyJ95tem0xArIn37N--JMIggZfZoC0UPAV6EZLKcr1-HsgzJJRP5fi_-snX5uIDnY-0DVuIqAzwUCB8R66xSsb279VrizqVnMbb7WD41NV6GcdillRKrWZEVgP4Hsryf_pcK05U07pktJ2I8PSD4AYJ-SfDkT7VGOS-3CCBp-eg6vxi-gJTvW2uRTsKKROKUXdR33t-Q4bN4e8LCtkZh4rndEtHwFkonFVjAoPhb3srGEMKJVZ5bQRg3JLFeHErueWSEgF8GuXkDlVJRAh_1Q6UFt8ItMAUue4tkd8%3D)** _Let's keep Alaska moving forward._ Sincerely,

body:
Hi there — I wanted to bump this email to the top of your inbox.
We are still hoping that 14 more Democrats from 95811 will step up and become 2026 Founding Donors of our statewide campaign to flip toss-up U.S. House seats from red to blue.
We can’t afford to wait until the last minute — not when there is so much at stake. We must invest in our Forever Organizing program and ensure we are on the ground in the pivotal districts that’ll decide the House Majority right now.
That’s why we are asking our supporters to become 2026 Founding Donors. Can I count on you? I am dropping my personal ActBlue link below, and I hope to see your donation come in.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-jan26?refcode=&amount= [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-jan26?refcode=&amount=]
Thank you,
– Camille
– – – – – Begin forwarded message – – – – –
In 2025, California shocked the entire country.
While institutions and politicians capitulated to Donald Trump, Gov. Gavin Newsom and the entire state of California dared to stand up and defy him .
When Trump tried to rig the 2026 Elections by asking Texas and other red states to redraw Congressional maps to eliminate blue districts, we struck back with Proposition 50 .
Thanks to an around-the-clock, rapid response effort, we foiled Trump’s election-rigging scheme and won by a landslide.
Now, we need to finish the job by taking our new maps and WINNING the new swing districts. If we can’t actually flip these seats from red to blue, all our work passing Proposition 50 will be for nothing.
That’s why we are launching our 2026 Founding Donor program and asking you to make your first donation in 2026 that’ll go toward flipping seats up and down our state. We are relying on at least 32 donors from your area. Are you in? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-jan26?refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-jan26?&refcode=&amount=]You might be thinking, “Hmm...it’s a little early to be making a donation with elections in November...”
But here at the California Democratic Party, we are committed to organizing and mobilizing in key districts around the clock, all year long. Why? That’s how we win.
Our Forever Organizing team is working every day to:
🚪 Knock on doors;
📋 Build coalitions;
☎️ Make phone calls;
📣 Get the word out about our people-powered Democratic candidates and the Republican incumbents who betrayed them in favor of serving Donald Trump.
Do you remember back in 2024 when we were waiting for WEEKS to figure out whether Democrats or Republicans would control the House? Most of those razor-thin races were here in California. And we flipped three of them from red to blue!
That’s why we are activating our 2026 Founding Donor program and asking you to invest in the team that will successfully flip these seats and the House Majority this year. Can we count on you? We are relying on at least 32 donors from your area to hit our target >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-jan26?refcode=&amount=]

Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action.
If this email was forwarded to you, please sign up for our email list here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/zWMh-KAhmUiJYxMzOxKADQ2]
We rely on email to communicate with supporters like you and conduct the business of the California Democratic Party, but if you wish to receive only our most important emails, let us know here to sign up for fewer emails. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/60txJAHUg0OznGGaWVwO_A2]
And if you no longer wish to receive emails from the California Democratic Party, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.cadem.org/p/BZcF64X0Zkie6fv4FEU9PQ2?unsubscribedata=VYZcQfh9kg3RKAqd0AiwDIwTvBQ3fxLl8A3UjFYAjo3FlkG%2FEwPPR5MGA59z4NnqWSuW8vfLk9s6i9worNy53EPAYoNERVJzG%2BppCMdFjhY1hLQ2DYkAalcl69U%2FUcjnVzf1aLPZiI%2BkeKqhmG2PViOws249Sr7cbH7lFE4Bc%2BN99NXqSygRgLZSI8byL1gdT7CbjzeFUSdVdILLAvE4HYeQidYsYTfLyFZFIPeCwlbTSfhGEh%2BYS7IyGQCRr6SYvgaVJXu89Rl7dk8u0PhAi2nNsmoFwG%2F%2FALK3%2B%2FbYlWfsSZgWlWSGVgqkYKdOfxJTMoS3Tokstg91ywCLU1gOfW%2BuQaIN9CHZDoGY5KD2UlEMr0EBu%2BvPz60%2BVrRIhPxpfqdQoKsT9bzBmzMk%2FTgvYpcPgmUCBqenPA3V6NjpIq8%3D .

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Court Hearing for DC Police Bodycam J6 Footage
body:
Accountability is Not ‘Political Retribution’ – It is Justice

[INSIDE JW]

ACCOUNTABILITY IS NOT ‘POLITICAL RETRIBUTION’ – IT IS JUSTICE

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36816&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj1MbVNZREFrVkVGOCZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=a92187ed7e221f9a3c8f74303914ae6e9eb9c146b527b8e52bfff773f3b0f03a]

In my column for _The Washington Times_, I write how the law must be
applied fairly:

> In America, the rule of law depends upon there being one system of
> justice for the entire country, but let’s be honest: Until now,
> that hasn’t been true.
>
> The Washington establishment has operated under a double standard:
> One for them and another for everyone else. James B. Comey
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36817&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnRpbWVzLmNvbS90b3BpY3MvamFtZXMtYi1jb21leS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=2f07458f6c1242ff57dcce2f92cb0a22de03f2c3b733ebc482b1f26586bc923a],
Letitia
> “Tish” James
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36818&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnRpbWVzLmNvbS90b3BpY3MvbGV0aXRpYS1qYW1lcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=b41f3794a1e15955a0364034b9cab73334dad7ca2baf0806e3296797d299f067]
and John R.
> Bolton
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36819&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnRpbWVzLmNvbS90b3BpY3Mvam9obi1yLWJvbHRvbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=8d3b12cf7e39095ae014c8d5ab1f1be414a7d9a86640eed4587508867da334fd],
all
> now targeted for or under indictment, are not victims of
> “retribution,” as the leftist media and Democrats loudly claim.
> They are facing long-overdue accountability for alleged criminal
> misconduct tied to abuse of power, deception and the weaponization
> of government against political opponents and the rule of law
> itself.
>
> The liberal media’s narrative of “revenge” is a convenient
> distraction. It ignores the years of documented misconduct that
> helped erode public trust in our institutions — much of which
> Judicial Watch exposed through litigation and investigations. These
> cases aren’t about politics; they are about restoring equal
> justice and integrity to the system that the political left
> corrupted.
>
> The indictment of Mr. Comey, a former FBI director, on charges of
> obstruction of a congressional proceeding and making false
> statements, should come as no surprise to those who have followed
> the man’s record. Judicial Watch uncovered evidence that the FBI
> retrieved personal memos from Mr. Comey after he left office, which
> he had either leaked or kept without authorization. We obtained
> documents showing that he coordinated with Robert Mueller’s team
> before his 2017 testimony and used his office to promote a baseless
> case against President Trump.
>
> Mr. Comey lied, leaked and obstructed, all to manipulate
> investigations and preserve the anti-Trump narrative. Now, when he
> is finally called to answer for those actions, his attorneys accuse
> Mr. Trump of “personal animus.” That’s nonsense. These charges
> stem from his own misconduct, not from politics. Accountability
> isn’t retribution; it’s the law doing what it should have done
> years ago.
>
> Ms. James made her political career by campaigning on the promise to
> “get Trump.” She smeared him as an “illegitimate president”
> and then weaponized her position as New York attorney general to
> pursue baseless civil charges against Mr. Trump and his company.
> Even a leftist-dominated appellate court in New York rejected Ms.
> James’ abuse of Mr. Trump and knocked out the abusive fines she
> sought.
>
> Now, when Ms. James herself faces charges of bank fraud and lying to
> a financial institution, the same media that cheered her partisan
> crusade portray her as a victim. It’s hypocrisy. The rule of law
> doesn’t stop applying when the accused is a Democrat with elite
> connections. For now, the courts have shut down the indictments of
> Mr. Comey and Ms. James for dubious reasons, but the underlying
> charges are rightly still being pursued.
>
> Then there’s the self-styled “guardian” of national security,
> Mr. Bolton, who, according to federal prosecutors, mishandled and
> unlawfully transmitted classified defense information more than a
> dozen times. More than 1,000 pages of national defense material
> leaked through his personal accounts, with reckless disregard for
> security. For years, Mr. Bolton
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36819&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud2FzaGluZ3RvbnRpbWVzLmNvbS90b3BpY3Mvam9obi1yLWJvbHRvbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=8d3b12cf7e39095ae014c8d5ab1f1be414a7d9a86640eed4587508867da334fd]
cast himself
> as a “truth teller” against Mr. Trump. Now, he faces 18 federal
> counts for behavior that placed real intelligence at risk.
>
> Everyone with a security clearance knows full well the consequences
> of publicizing classified information. Mr. Bolton apparently thought
> the law didn’t apply to him. Once again, the “system” is
> finally holding a powerful insider accountable.
>
> Mr. Trump’s critics, now joined by the same media that cheered
> lawfare against him for years, claim these indictments mark a
> dangerous precedent. Yet the real danger lies in pretending that
> elites such as Messrs. Comey and Bolton and Ms. James are above the
> law. For years, they targeted citizens and political opponents using
> the machinery of justice as a weapon. They normalized selective
> prosecution. The current cases simply return the law to its
> rightful, impartial place.
>
> Abigail Jackson, spokeswoman for the Trump White House, put it
> plainly in September: “It is the highest form of hypocrisy for
> Democrats and the media to falsely claim accountability is
> ‘political retribution’” after years of weaponizing the entire
> Biden administration. When one side systematically abuses power,
> restoring accountability will always look like retribution to those
> who benefited from corruption.
>
> Sunlight is the best disinfectant. The law must apply equally,
> whether your name is Donald Trump, James Comey or John Bolton. Mr.
> Trump’s administration is not seeking revenge; it is repairing
> justice. That restoration is not tyranny. It is the republic’s
> renewal.

COURT HEARING HELD FOR DC POLICE BODYCAM FOOTAGE FROM JANUARY 6, 2021

We were in court yesterday for a hearing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36803&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGMtamFuLTYtYm9keWNhbS1oZWFyaW5nLTAwMzQ1My8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=be64691ba0fb6a86c304b4fba02c5180f95cbc038914b5a3eb0d0007d5266f0e]
before D.C. Superior Court Judge Carl E. Ross in a Freedom of
Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit against the District of Columbia for
local police bodycam footage from January 6, 2021.

We filed the June 2024 FOIA lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=32956&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGMtcG9saWNlLWJvZHljYW0tdmlkZW9zLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=e69c615d7e59ff03b61359b65794da52671752484aa2822a739628a6041553ee]
in the U.S.
District Court for the District of Columbia after the Metropolitan
Police Department (MPD) denied our August 2021 request (_Judicial
Watch v. District of Columbia_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=32934&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGMtYm9keWNhbS1mb290YWdlLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDM0NTMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=c93a09ccf2cc4f03f7c391db4f7acf6f506c690b32486c8845b608e1102b79d8]
(No. 2024-CAB-003453)). We are seeking all footage captured by the
body-worn cameras of police department officers who responded to the
Capitol on January 6.

The Metropolitan Police Department continues to withhold the January 6
footage because it claims that the faces and voices of every
individual who is not law enforcement personnel in the videos for
personal privacy reasons. Because of this, the police department seeks
to charge
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=34692&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGMtcG9saWNlLXdhbnQtb3Zlci0xbS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e6d4a5cb8285700e7afac2822cf660c4df01b57d2e0e238ce435f527061e0c85]
us
more than $1.5 million so that the police department can redact those
faces and voices in the videos, which supposedly contain over one
thousand hours of footage.

In a September 2025 filing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36804&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGMtamFuLTYtYm9keWNhbS1yZXNwb25zZS0wMDM0NTMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6c3d5ad2ed246feedc8e667cde780f7cb3bbe98444b50317baae2095872ccb9e],
we argue that DC’s claim of FOIA’s personal privacy redaction
requirement is wrong because no privacy interest exists – and even
if it did exist any privacy interest is outweighed by the public’s
interest in this case:

> [Metropolitan Police Department’s bodycam] footage from January 6,
> 2021 captured a public event on public property. The footage does
> not reveal private financial information, hospital admission
> records, social security numbers, medical records or similar
> materials. The [bodycam] footage only consists of faces and voices
> that were seen and/or heard by anyone present on public property or
> has seen other images/videos captured that day. There is nothing
> whatsoever private about the faces and voices....

> If the Court were to find that more than a _de minimis _privacy
> interest is implicated by the unredacted disclosure of the [bodycam]
> footage, the obvious public interest outweighs it.... [L]ike the
> release of footage from the Capitol surveillance cameras, the
> release of [the police department’s bodycam] footage “will
> provide millions of Americans, criminal defendants, public interest
> organizations, and the media an ability to see for themselves what
> happened that day, rather than having to rely upon the
> interpretation of a small group of government officials.” See X
> post here
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36805&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly94LmNvbS9TcGVha2VySm9obnNvbi9zdGF0dXMvMTcyNTYyODI3NDY1NzcwNjE5OD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=93bfd75f83ac1f38744f3a28f91589ec1f80095f1216f24097bbaf822e75b17d].

There is no good reason to hide these videos from January 6, and we
will continue our fight five years later for the public’s right to
see the unedited footage.

We have extensively investigated the events of January 6.

In July 2025, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36077&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGFzdC1taW51dGUtajYtdGFyZ2V0aW5nLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=0435e432bc35f476912f79a4cbd894717ea305ccfac170f1aa6edb486f362342]
the U.S.
Department of Justice for records on accelerated January 6
prosecutions after Donald Trump was elected president in November
2024. The Biden administration, anticipating President-elect Donald
Trump’s promise to issue pardons for January 6, 2021, defendants, is
believed to have accelerated prosecutions in the final months of
Biden’s term.

In August 2025, we announced that the U.S. Air Force would finally
provide full military funeral honors
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=35989&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZnVuZXJhbC1ob25vcnMtYXNobGktYmFiYml0dC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=238d6da3fc522617b83f0f5ba9a1a2f959fb37351b5cd24d9e71224ef4f9a75c]
to Ashli
Babbitt, the Air Force veteran who was shot and killed inside the U.S.
Capitol by then-Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd on January 6, 2021.
Babbitt was the only official January 6 homicide victim. The Biden
administration had previously denied Babbitt and her family these
honors in retaliation for being at the U.S. Capitol that day. This
decision came on the heels of a massive, nearly $5 million Trump
administration settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=35379&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvNC05NzUtbWlsbGlvbi1zZXR0bGVtZW50LW9mLWFzaGxpLWJhYmJpdHQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=1156af566a79604da3473b2dff32085037134316fdf9b21e745c7773c7f59cce]
with her family for wrongful death and other claims against the U.S.
Government.

Also in August, a Georgia state court issued an order
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=35957&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZmFuaS13aWxsaXMtdHJ1bXAtcmVjb3Jkcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=5c5031b6fcfc0ff4dfd7f090d0ab70214dd8bf20c74bcea5ed455c27622d43c3]
in our
lawsuit for Georgia District Attorney Fani Willis to provide new
information and potentially conduct a new search for Trump-related
records because her affidavit to the court made no reference to
whether any searches of the devices of former Fulton County Special
Prosecutor Nathan Wade or those of Chief Investigator Michael L. Hill,
who was involved gathering evidence and coordinating investigative
efforts, and likely met
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=35952&F=T&&hash=9fbc743da6fc9d687b6929f69b9e729419b47b40896a679a6760792a426de17b]
with the January 6 Committee.

In May 2025, we sued

MEDICAID MAKES $289 MILLION IN “UNALLOWABLE PAYMENTS” TO DEAD
PEOPLE

Recent events in Minnesota have made it clear that your tax dollars
are carelessly tossed hither and yon. This is particularly true with
Medicaid, as our _Corruption Chronicles_ blog reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36813&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbWVkaWNhaWQtMjg5bS11bmFsbG93YWJsZS1wYXltZW50cy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=7d8a3d91ac0da70680ba5460a72a41bff4a302014e8af74536030dca77434728].

> A decade after Judicial Watch reported
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36820&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbWVkaWNhaWQtYmxldy0yNi1taWwtaGVhbHRoLWNvdmVyYWdlLWRlYWQtcGVvcGxlLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=9c67c872a883627875c703dc054a22a233af979db1800662867e3bceb6750d8f]
> that Medicaid, the government’ s fraud-infested health insurance
> program for the needy, spent $26 million to provide dead people in
> one state alone with benefits, a federal audit reveals the problem
> continues full-throttle with hundreds of millions of dollars in
> “unallowable payments” on behalf of “deceased enrollees.” It
> is an unbelievable story that illustrates government inefficiency,
> especially when it involves welfare programs. The waste has been
> well documented for many years, yet little has been done to correct
> the problem. With an annual budget of about $900 billion Medicaid is
> jointly funded by federal and state governments. The Centers for
> Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) administers the program at the
> federal level and states have CMS-approved plans to run their
> Medicaid programs, which provide low-income residents with medical
> coverage. In some states the government contracts with insurance
> companies and makes fixed monthly payments to provide coverage.
>
> For years, the payments have continued even after the patient died.
> In other cases, millions of dollars of “unallowable payments”
> are made on behalf of incarcerated individuals, who lose Medicaid
> benefits once they are imprisoned because healthcare is provided by
> correction facilities. In a recent example, Illinois spent at least
> $9.5 million
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36821&F=T&&hash=cc4c153774e7a8b20c5f627d8e22223b72df91feda9ab19eed3b125b8982bb29]
> to provide ineligible inmates, who had been enrolled in Medicaid
> prior to incarceration, with medical benefits. As in many states,
> the Illinois Department of Corrections Prison Data Match
> collaborates with Medicaid Managed Care Enrollment Termination to
> provide a weekly list of incarcerated individuals, and the
> information is matched against the state’s Medicaid enrollment
> data to identify incarcerated enrollees, so Medicaid benefits are
> terminated. Although the system has been in place nationwide for
> years, a recent Health and Human Services (HHS) Inspector General
> (IG) probe examined Illinois records and determined that, between
> 2020 and 2023, at least $9.5 million in unallowable payments were
> made on behalf of imprisoned Medicaid recipients because the state
> failed to terminate managed care enrollment after being notified of
> incarceration.
>
> The failure is similar involving dead people, though the price tag
> is much higher. Since 2016 the HHS IG has conducted 18 audits
> identifying that Medicaid agencies improperly made about $289
> million
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36822&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9vaWcuaGhzLmdvdi9kb2N1bWVudHMvYXVkaXQvMTE0MzMvQS0wNC0yMy0wOTAxMC5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=17f8f86f1d6eec382e60c97617cf170d6d6303f96451db727c7b5c9a1d89da7f]
in
> payments on behalf of recipients after they died. Even after all
> these years, “states continue to struggle with the issue,”
> according to the recently published audit. Investigators found that
> Medicaid agencies throughout the country doled out the money for the
> healthcare of deceased beneficiaries even though the deaths were
> officially recorded in the Social Security Administration’s Death
> Master File. In most cases the cash kept flowing, the HHS watchdog
> confirms, writing that “we determined that Medicaid agencies made
> unallowable capitation payments after enrollees’ deaths for 99 of
> the 100 sample payments.” Some of the overpayments were recovered
> by the government but the overwhelming majority were not, sticking
> it to American taxpayers as so many bloated welfare programs
> regularly do.
>
> Improper payments have been a long-standing and significant problem
> in the federal government, according to the Government
> Accountability Office (GAO), the investigative arm of Congress, and
> Medicaid is among the biggest offenders. In fiscal year 2024 the GAO
> found that Medicaid made an estimated $31.1 billion
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36823&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ2FvLmdvdi9hc3NldHMvZ2FvLTI1LTEwNzc3MC5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e2335ac0e6ae4237208df3c2b9dc156c91787cc5005c455b67170f9c45988d3e]
in improper payments,
> which is equal to about five percent of the program’s total
> spending. A year earlier the HHS watchdog reported that 14 states
> made over $249 million in unallowable payments on behalf of dead
> people with 11 of those failing to consistently identify and process
> beneficiaries’ death information. A few years ago, an Oregon state
> audit revealed that $445 million in Medicaid benefits were paid to
> beneficiaries enrolled in one or more other states, including tens
> of millions for people covered under California and Washington
> state’s Medicaid program.

NIH TO RECONSIDER FROZEN DEI, GENDER IDENTITY GRANTS WORTH MILLIONS

Feeding at the federal trough continues, with various leftwing groups
and officials fighting in court to retain a grab bag of questionable
grants. Our _Corruption Chronicles_ blog explains
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36824&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbmloLXJlY29uc2lkZXItZnJvemVuLWRlaS1ncmFudHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=75288a94e40c148d4a333994868fbbf44f283794ab4fdc851daab57e496e0fdf].

> A portion of diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) grants frozen or
> denied by the National Institutes of Health (NIH) last year will be
> reviewed and possibly reinstated by the Trump administration to
> appease the left. Worth hundreds of millions of dollars, the
> rejected grants also fund research related to LGBTQ+ and transgender
> issues as well as gender identity, areas of study that do not meet
> the administration’s priorities. That is why last spring the NIH,
> which invests most of its $48 billion budget
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36825&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmloLmdvdi9hYm91dC1uaWgvb3JnYW5pemF0aW9uL2J1ZGdldD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=8aadfd0e5ffb55a6adb491b74bc765beee1a8045646a60d9295274e202ebad5f]

>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36826&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmloLmdvdi9hYm91dC1uaWgvb3JnYW5pemF0aW9uL2J1ZGdldF1pbj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b2ad72259bdbebb5a42696cb5504c64283248ce54133afe6f0db66d16b8c9a95
medical
> research, nixed grants flagged for DEI, transgender, LGBTQ+ and
> other leftist projects that received large sums of taxpayer dollars
> under Biden. Some were buried among the 50,000 grants the NIH awards
> annually to more than 300,000 researchers at over 2,500 universities
> and medical schools nationwide, but the Trump administration plucked
> them out and revoked them. Thousands of grants worth billions of
> dollars were targeted over DEI, gender identity and LGBTQ+ research.
>
> Predictably, legal action ensued and several lawsuits were filed by
> researchers, unions, civil rights groups and a coalition of
> Democratic states that saw $783 million in frozen DEI grants. That
> federal lawsuit, filed by attorneys general in Massachusetts,
> California, Maryland, Washington, Arizona, Colorado, Delaware,
> Hawaii, Minnesota, Nevada, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York, Oregon,
> Rhode Island and Wisconsin alleges the Trump administration violated
> the U.S. Constitution and the Administrative Procedure Act by
> unreasonably delaying the review and disposition of applications for
> NIH grants submitted by researchers. Another lawsuit
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36827&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuaGlnaGVyZWRkaXZlLmNvbS9uZXdzL2ZlZGVyYWwtanVkZ2Utc3RyaWtlcy1kb3duLW5paC1kaXJlY3RpdmVzLWFnYWluc3QtZGVpLXJlc2VhcmNoLzc1MDk2OC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=64d2c059e0746896ee05a8b2a7247710c8df49bc83d2f909fab043e838a05f17],
> filed by a leftwing civil right group, says a plaintiff whose
> research focuses on sexual violence in minority communities had six
> grants terminated and another that studied the promotion of mental
> and physical health among black men. An Ivy League professor lost
> her NIH grant focusing on obstetrical outcomes for lesbian, gay,
> bisexual and queer women, while another frozen award funded research
> on the mental health impacts of laws targeting the LGBTQ+
> population.
>
> In the case filed by the states, a trial court and an appeals court
> in Massachusetts sided with the attorneys general, but the U.S.
> Supreme Court later ruled
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36828&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8yNHBkZi8yNWExMDNfa2g3cC5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4937dcdb1382ee520f08d27ad823fe1085c2a819f86c1e33ec1b6481606844a7]

>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36829&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8yNHBkZi8yNWExMDNfa2g3cC5wZGZddGhhdD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=ca5f275494f2004623ab34c200cf1e15abda5a9c52c6b3c608e6c689eabe8690
> the trial judge lacked the authority to compel the grants to be
> paid. The states asked the Supreme Court to leave the lower
> court’s order in place, claiming that the termination of the DEI
> grants “caused unrecoverable loss of scientific knowledge” and
> would inflict incalculable losses in public health and human life.
> The high court did not agree with its 5-4 decision setting aside the
> lower court rulings and allowing the Trump administration to cancel
> hundreds of millions of dollars in NIH grants involving DEI. The
> ruling certainly makes it tougher for grant recipients who challenge
> the Trump administration’s changes to federal funding based on its
> new DEI and gender identity policies. The Supreme Court decision
> undoubtedly gives the government the upper hand to withhold taxpayer
> dollars for research that involves wasteful projects prioritized by
> the previous administration.
>
> That makes the Trump administration’s decision to review and
> possibly reinstate the canceled DEI grants puzzling. In a settlement
> agreement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36830&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubWFzcy5nb3YvZG9jL25paC0yLWFncmVlbWVudC9kb3dubG9hZD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=467832bf6ea6b2d98583f171f24a4d45d764f40ecd764a0f85aec0672a75d6c9]
> recently signed by the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the attorneys
> general who sued the administration, the NIH agrees to consider
> hundreds of grants worth $783 million without taking into account
> DEI. The document was filed in Massachusetts federal court on Dec.
> 29 and the state’s attorney general, Andrea Joy Campbell, calls it
> a victory against unlawful directives
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78842&L=36831&F=T&&hash=7bcfaea9e21c43eb140e69294d6c2170a31d7cb30cac0d3a821677f35f1aab6e]
> that targeted NIH projects based on their perceived connection to
> DEI, transgender issues, vaccine hesitancy and other topics
> disfavored by the Trump administration. Under the agreement NIH
> officials “will complete their consideration of the Applications
> in the ordinary course of NIH’s scientific review process, without
> applying the Challenged Directives.” This clearly refers to the
> Trump administration’s ban on funding controversial DEI,
> transgender and gender identity research. The NIH also commits to
> “evaluate each application individually and in good faith.”
